meta_title: For Students: testing automation to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to testing automation and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Testing Automation to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to use testing automation to overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to build real-world skills.
content.answer_block: Overcome the 'no portfolio' barrier by building automated testing suites for open-source projects. By leveraging Antigravity for test generation and Claude Code for implementation, you demonstrate professional-grade engineering discipline. This tangible proof of quality code separates you from average applicants, signaling you are ready for production environments.
content.intro:
The silence of an empty portfolio is deafening when you are staring down your first job application. You have the skills, but you lack the battle scars that prove you can handle production-grade software. The industry doesn't just want code that works; they want code that survives the chaos of real-world usage.

Testing automation is your secret weapon. By shifting your focus from 'just making it work' to 'proving it stays working,' you transition from a student to an engineer. Using modern AI tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can now construct robust testing frameworks that demonstrate the architectural maturity recruiters are desperately seeking.

steps.title: Analyze the Target Repository
steps.description: Identify a small open-source project and use Claude Code to map its dependencies and critical failure points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this codebase and identify the 3 most critical functions that lack unit test coverage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'map the dependency graph of the current directory'

steps.title: Scaffold the Test Infrastructure
steps.description: Set up a testing environment using a standard framework like Vitest or PyTest to ensure your tests run consistently.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a boilerplate test configuration file for the identified critical functions using PyTest.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize a standard testing directory structure'

steps.title: Generate Intelligent Test Cases
steps.description: Use AI to write edge-case scenarios that would break a novice developer's code, such as empty inputs or race conditions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create 5 unit tests for the user authentication module, including negative test cases for invalid input types.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate unit tests based on the project structure'

steps.title: Implement Continuous Integration
steps.description: Automate the execution of your tests so they run every time code is pushed, proving your workflow is professional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a GitHub Actions YAML file to automate running these tests on every pull request.

steps.title: Document the Impact
steps.description: Update the README to highlight how your testing suite improved code reliability, making it a portfolio centerpiece.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional README section explaining the testing strategy, coverage metrics, and the benefits of the new CI pipeline.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'update README.md to include a testing section'

pro_tips: Focus on 'Mutation Testing'—it proves your tests are actually checking the right things by intentionally injecting bugs.
Always document the 'Why' in your commit messages, not just the 'What'.
Use Claude Code to refactor legacy code *after* your tests pass to show you understand the refactoring cycle.
Add a 'Test Coverage Badge' to your GitHub repo; it provides instant visual credibility to recruiters.
Target projects with low test coverage; your contribution becomes significantly more valuable to the maintainers.
content.next_steps: Once your tests are live, contribute these as a Pull Request to the original project. The real-world feedback from maintainers is more valuable than any degree.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a contributor building a production-grade legacy. Stop waiting for permission to be an engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
